facade_description: 51 Oxford St presents a row-house facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 3 storeys and is capped by a gable, consistent with its house-form, row, ontario cottage typology. The facade reads primarily as brick, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 2, 1 window bays from lower to upper levels, using double-hung sash windows, and 1 principal entrance. Character-defining facade elements include cornice, decorative brick, original windows, bay window. HCD note: Each property has value as a nineteenth-century Ontario Cottage House-form building that reflects early workers’ housing within the District.
